raw_consumer_statement:
I disputed a {$640.00} charge on my Citi credit card made by XXXX XXXX XXXX  ( XX/XX/year> ). This charge was the result of a bait-and-switch scheme. 

I had already prepaid {$320.00} for the same rental through XXXX on XX/XX/year>, using my XXXX XXXX  ( Confirmation # : XXXX ), including XXXX XXXX XXXX. When I arrived to pick up the vehicle, XXXX  refused to honor the reservation or accept my insurance. They told me I would not receive the car unless I paid again at a much higher price using my Citi card. I had no other options and paid under duress. 

The {$640.00} Citi charge was : A duplicate payment for the same rental Coerced under threat of being denied the vehicle Inflated with undisclosed fees and forced insurance I provided Citi with full documentation : The original XXXX reservation and payment A copy of the forced Citi invoice Proof of the XXXX payment XXXX XXXX reviews showing that XXXX XXXX XXXX  has a well-documented pattern of similar behavior Citi denied my dispute, saying the rental numbers didnt match and no credit slip was issued. However, this ignores the nature of the charge it was fraudulent, coercive, and misrepresented, not a normal transaction. I was charged twice for the same service and was not given what I originally paid for. 

Citi failed to protect me under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( 15 U.S. Code 1666 ), which allows disputes for services not delivered as agreed or unauthorized charges. I believe they did not properly investigate or apply this law. 

I am requesting that the CFPB review how Citi handled this dispute and require them to reopen it and reverse the charge.
